text: Midline sternotomy was created.
Palpation of the mediastinum showed a mass extending along the right border of the pericardium into the pleural space.
The pericardium was opened medially to this beginning at the innominate vein.
Within the pleural sac, there was some evidence of caseous material.
There was no gross bloody hemorrhage or other material noted.
Medial dissection of the pericardium extended down to the diaphragmatic surface.
Caseous material can be seen within the pericardium.
The mass protrudes medially and compresses the right atrium.
After opening the pericardium, the superior vena cava, right atrium, and aorta are clearly visualized.
We next opened widely the right pleural space for evaluation, and this showed that the right upper lobe of the lung was adherent to the mass.
Using electrocautery, the lung was then removed from the anterior surface of the mass.
Lung tissue did not appear nodular or irregular, either by visual inspection or palpation.
After taking down some adhesions, we can appreciate the anterior surface of the extracardiac mass.
We then began to move along the lateral edge of the mass.
This extended down to and very near the phrenic nerve.
Throughout the dissection, the mass was in close association with the phrenic nerve along its anatomic course to the diaphragm.
Similarly, the inferior margin of the mass in pericardium was incised, and this too was dissected using electrocautery to join our previous dissection superiorly.
Once these adhesions were taken down, we began to circumferentially dissect the mass.
Note the caseous material emanating from the mass during inferior dissection.
Note the mature hair tissue extending from the teratoma.
The cystic components of the mass began to drain out before it was completely removed.
Delicate dissection was carried out near the right atrium.
The final adhesions were taken down and the mass was excised.
The mass was oriented with suture material and sent to pathology.
The remnant of the thymic tissue in the superior anterior mediastinum was removed in its entirety as well.
Here we can see that the right phrenic nerve has been preserved after resection of the mass as it courses to the diaphragm.
Medium and large surgical clips were used to mark the tumor margins along the pericardial edge and the pleural reflection from our excision of the mass.
Due to the case use material found within the mediastinum, the chest was irrigated with two liters of sterile saline and suctioned dry.
Sterile water was brought to the surgical field, and one liter of this was instilled within the chest cavity and agitated for cell lysis.
Here we can see the pericardial, superior, right thoracic, and inferior margins of the mass in addition to the resected pericardium.
Suture orientation of the mass is noted above.
Pathologic examination confirmed a mature cystic teratoma and benign thymic tissue.
All malignant cell markers were negative and all cultures were negative as well.
